name: stage2-rvv-low-precision-contraction-surface
title: Stage2 RVV low-precision contraction primitive-surface campaign
description: Macro campaign for Stage2 RVV low-precision contraction primitive-surface maturity. Current slice quarantines policy/evidence/admission/result fields away from stable low-precision resource and primitive compiler authority by introducing a stable resource compiler-fact view for route planning and target validation.
creator: codex
notes: Foundation outcome completed: the low-precision primitive typed surface and provider validation cover signed i8 and unsigned u8 product-reduction facts. Selected-body realization outcome completed for bounded signed and unsigned representatives: tcrv_rvv.typed_widening_product_reduce_pre_realized_body carries source_signedness, verifier/provider validation accepts only signedness-consistent product-reduction contracts, and RVV selected-body realization materializes explicit setvl/with_vl/load/widening_product/standalone_reduce/store structure before route construction. Provider/artifact carry-through outcome completed for the plain signed/unsigned product-reduction representative slice: route descriptions carry a provider-owned low-precision primitive route payload, emission metadata serializes tcrv_rvv.low_precision_primitive.* only from that payload, and target artifact validation compares candidate primitive mirrors against the provider payload before artifact acceptance. Recent packed-i4 policy-boundary cleanup separated load/unpack, realization, schedule, resource-cost, runtime ABI, and target facts from realization-admission, remediation planning, performance feedback, admission, maturity, dispatch preference, and same-target evidence. Gearbox stable resource-schedule canonicalization is complete: RVVLowPrecisionPackedI4StableResourceScheduleFacts owns the accepted packed-i4 schedule-decision, unroll/accumulator/region/live-vector budget, and resource-cost facts consumed by Gearbox candidate selection, handoff schedule verification, provider route planning, and target artifact validation. Selected-body realization admission/evidence boundary cleanup is complete: provider realization compiler-fact gates validate typed/resource/realization, packed load/unpack, Gearbox handoff, stable schedule, and resource-cost facts without consuming packed-i4 admission/remediation/performance/measurement/no-win/dispatch fields as route, schedule, or resource acceptance facts. Provider primitive route-payload canonicalization is complete: RVV provider plan/description validation and target provider-facts validation call the provider-owned verifyRVVLowPrecisionPrimitiveRoutePayloadFromWideningReductionFacts helper, so source dtype/signedness, load/extension, product/accumulator/final-result dtype, SEW/LMUL, policy, runtime AVL/VL, product-reduction relation, intrinsics, seed, layout, and store-VL payload fields must mirror provider widening-reduction primitive facts before target mirror validation. Support-bundle/export primitive mirror-boundary cleanup is complete: RVV target support-bundle header evidence groups low-precision primitive header comments under low_precision_primitive.payload_mirror.* while candidate metadata keys remain tcrv_rvv.low_precision_primitive.* for provider-payload validation, and target tests reject missing primitive export mirrors instead of inferring primitive semantics from adjacent metadata. Route-description/emission-plan/candidate primitive mirror-boundary cleanup is complete: legacy route-description scalar primitive fields are populated from the provider-built RVVLowPrecisionPrimitiveRoutePayload, emission metadata uses a single payload-mirror helper and payload-sourced product-reduction relation/intrinsic/layout/store-VL mirrors, candidate metadata carries tcrv_rvv.low_precision_primitive.payload_mirror_source = provider-built-low-precision-primitive-route-payload.v1, and target validation rejects missing or stale payload-mirror source markers before accepting primitive candidate mirrors. Low-precision resource-owner mirror-source cleanup is complete: emission metadata carries tcrv_rvv.low_precision_resource.resource_owner_mirror_source = provider-owned-low-precision-contraction-resource-selection.v1 for routes with provider-owned resource selections, target candidate validation rejects missing or stale resource-owner markers before accepting stable low-precision resource mirrors, and support-bundle/header evidence exposes low_precision_resource.resource_owner_mirror.source as transport evidence while keeping candidate metadata keys unchanged. Latest slice completed low-precision mirror transport contract consolidation: RVVLowPrecisionMirrorTransportContract now provides the primitive payload and resource-owner marker key/source/header/diagnostic labels; route planning appends marker metadata through makeRVVLowPrecisionMirrorSourceMetadata; support-bundle/header evidence consumes the same contract and checks exact provider-owned marker source values; target artifact validation and C++ missing/stale marker fixtures consume the same contract before accepting primitive/resource mirrors. Macro task remains open for adjacent low-precision primitive/resource cleanup and future fresh source-backed same-target measurement-disposition work if later claimed. Next continuation point: inspect remaining low-precision primitive/resource mirror surfaces for duplicated transport strings or acceptance decisions, then continue to fresh source-backed measurement-disposition only if a later slice claims performance or dispatch preference. Current resource-selection policy/evidence quarantine slice introduced RVVLowPrecisionStableResourceCompilerFacts as the policy-free stable compiler-fact view consumed by route planning and target stable resource mirror validation; realization-admission, remediation, performance, measurement, no-win/maturity, and dispatch-preference fields remain explicit policy/evidence mirrors and do not satisfy primitive payload, stable resource, schedule, route, support-bundle, or artifact acceptance. Next continuation point: inspect remaining low-precision support-bundle or artifact acceptance paths for broad aggregate consumption versus stable compiler-fact views or explicitly named policy/evidence validators.
